These Amended and Restated Bylaws of Terravox Systems, Inc. (the \"Corporation\" or the \"Company\"), a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware, were originally adopted on March 14, 2016, and are hereby amended and restated effective as of November 3, 2021. These Bylaws are adopted pursuant to the provisions of the Delaware General Corporation Law (the \"DGCL\") and the Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation of Terravox Systems, Inc. (the \"Certificate of Incorporation\").

**[ARTICLE I --- OFFICES]{.underline}**

**Section 1.1 --- Registered Office**

The registered office of the Corporation in the State of Delaware shall be as set forth in the Certificate of Incorporation. The Corporation may also have offices at such other places, both within and outside the State of Delaware, as the Board of Directors may from time to time determine or as the business of the Corporation may require.

**Section 1.2 --- Principal Office**

The principal office of the Corporation shall be located at 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723, or at such other place as the Board of Directors shall from time to time designate.

**[ARTICLE II --- STOCKHOLDERS]{.underline}**

**Section 2.1 --- Annual Meetings**

The annual meeting of stockholders for the election of directors and for the transaction of such other business as may properly come before the meeting shall be held at such date, time, and place (if any) as shall be determined by the Board of Directors and stated in the notice of the meeting. The Board may, in its sole discretion, determine that an annual meeting shall be held solely by means of remote communication in accordance with Section 211(a)(2) of the DGCL.

**Section 2.2 --- Special Meetings**

Special meetings of the stockholders may be called at any time by (a) the Board of Directors pursuant to a resolution adopted by a majority of the total number of directors then in office, (b) the Chairman of the Board, or (c) holders of at least twenty-five percent (25%) of the outstanding shares of voting stock of the Corporation entitled to vote generally in the election of directors. Special meetings may not be called by any other person or persons. Business transacted at any special meeting of stockholders shall be limited to the purposes stated in the notice of such meeting.

**Section 2.3 --- Notice of Meetings**

Written notice of each meeting of stockholders, whether annual or special, stating the date, time, place (if any), the means of remote communication (if any) by which stockholders may be deemed to be present in person and vote, and, in the case of a special meeting, the purpose or purposes for which the meeting is called, shall be given not less than ten (10) nor more than sixty (60) days before the date of the meeting to each stockholder entitled to vote at such meeting. Notice may be given by electronic transmission in accordance with Section 232 of the DGCL.

**Section 2.4 --- Quorum**

At each meeting of stockholders, except where otherwise required by law, the Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws, the holders of a majority of the outstanding shares of stock entitled to vote at such meeting, present in person or represented by proxy, shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business. In the absence of a quorum, the holders of a majority of the shares present in person or represented by proxy and entitled to vote may adjourn the meeting from time to time without notice other than announcement at the meeting, until a quorum shall be present.

**Section 2.5 --- Voting**

Except as otherwise provided by the DGCL, the Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws, each stockholder shall be entitled to one vote for each share of capital stock held by such stockholder. Except as otherwise provided by the DGCL, the Certificate of Incorporation, or these Bylaws, all matters brought before a meeting of stockholders shall be decided by the affirmative vote of the holders of a majority of the shares present in person or represented by proxy at the meeting and entitled to vote on the matter. Directors shall be elected by a plurality of the votes cast. Voting at meetings of stockholders need not be by written ballot unless the Board of Directors, in its discretion, or the officer of the Corporation presiding at the meeting, in such officer\'s discretion, shall so require.

**Section 2.6 --- Action by Written Consent**

Unless otherwise provided in the Certificate of Incorporation, any action required or permitted to be taken at any annual or special meeting of stockholders of the Corporation may be taken without a meeting, without prior notice, and without a vote, if a consent or consents in writing, setting forth the action so taken, shall be signed by the holders of outstanding stock having not less than the minimum number of votes that would be necessary to authorize or take such action at a meeting at which all shares entitled to vote thereon were present and voted. """Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

# PRIVILEGED & CONFIDENTIAL --- ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT

**PREPARED IN ANTICIPATION OF LITIGATION / FOR DEAL NEGOTIATION**

## MEMORANDUM

**TO:** Elena Marchetti; Jonathan Hsieh **FROM:** \[Associate\], Whitfield & Crane LLP **DATE:** October 28, 2024 **RE:** Governance Diligence --- Proposed Acquisition of Terravox Systems, Inc. by Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP

### I. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

This memorandum summarizes our review of the governance documents of Terravox Systems, Inc. ("Terravox" or the "Company") in connection with the proposed acquisition of 100% of the Company's outstanding equity by Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP ("Hargrove" or "Buyer"), structured as a reverse triangular merger at an enterprise value of \$485 million (equity value approximately \$473 million, net of approximately \$12 million of outstanding indebtedness). The documents reviewed are: (i) the Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation, dated November 3, 2021 (the "Restated Certificate" or "Charter"); (ii) the Amended and Restated Bylaws, effective November 3, 2021 (the "Bylaws"); (iii) the Stockholders' Agreement, dated November 3, 2021 (the "Stockholders' Agreement" or "SHA"); (iv) the Voting Agreement, dated November 3, 2021 (the "Voting Agreement"); (v) the Registration Rights Agreement, dated November 3, 2021 (the "RRA"); and (vi) Board minutes for eight meetings spanning January 2023 through September 2024 (the "Board Minutes").

**The central finding --- and the client's overriding concern --- is that Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP ("Stonemill") holds a hard, charter-level blocking right over the merger that cannot be satisfied without its affirmative consent.** Stonemill holds 100% of the outstanding Series C Preferred Stock (7,800,000 shares). The Charter's Series C separate-class protective provision (Section 4.3.6(b)) requires the consent of holders of at least 66⅔% of the Series C Preferred Stock, voting as a separate class, to effect any "Liquidation Event" --- and the reverse triangular merger squarely qualifies as a Liquidation Event. Because Stonemill is the *only* holder of Series C Preferred Stock, the 66⅔% threshold (at least 5,200,000 shares) cannot be reached without Stonemill. Julian Trask, Stonemill's board designee, confirmed this on the record at the September 25, 2024 Board meeting. This is the single most important fact in the diligence record.

**A secondary, but critical, finding is that the Stockholders' Agreement drag-along can be *triggered* without Stonemill, but whether it can *override* Stonemill's charter veto is a contested question of Delaware law.** The drag-along's three-pronged trigger can be satisfied with Ridgeline Ventures Fund II, LP ("Ridgeline") and Northbridge Growth Equity Fund IV, LP ("Northbridge") (and the common holders) alone --- Stonemill's participation is not required to reach the trigger thresholds. If validly triggered, the drag-along (reinforced by an irrevocable proxy) would purport to compel Stonemill to vote its Series C shares in favor of the merger, which would in turn satisfy the Series C separate-class vote. Delaware courts have enforced drag-along provisions against objecting stockholders (most notably *NHS Systems, Inc. v. Stericycle, Inc.*, Del. Ch. 2020), but the specific question of whether a contractual drag-along can compel a stockholder to satisfy a charter-level separate-class veto has not been definitively resolved. Stonemill would almost certainly litigate and could seek injunctive relief that delays the targeted Q1 2025 closing. **We recommend that the client treat the Series C separate-class consent as a hard requirement and negotiate to obtain it, while preserving the drag-along as leverage and a fallback --- not as the primary path to closing.**

**Economically, Stonemill has little incentive to support a higher headline price.** The Series C Preferred Stock is non-participating with a liquidation preference of \$18.50 per share (\$144.3 million aggregate). At the implied equity value of approximately \$473 million, the as-converted value of the Series C (approximately \$13.95 per share on a simple average, and approximately \$12.59 per share after payment of the Series C preference in the waterfall) is *below* the \$18.50 preference. Stonemill therefore receives its \$144.3 million preference regardless of price up to an enterprise value of approximately \$639 million (the conversion "crossover"). Within the current deal range, Stonemill captures no upside from a higher price. This explains why Hargrove's intelligence indicates Stonemill may seek value *outside* the merger waterfall --- a side arrangement, a higher allocation of consideration, or post-closing management rights --- rather than simply a higher headline price. This economic profile should inform the negotiation strategy.

**Three additional issues require immediate attention:**

1.  **SensorLink Technologies LOI --- potential live exclusivity (CRITICAL, time-sensitive).** The SensorLink LOI (executed March 8, 2024) contained a mutual exclusivity provision with an initial six-month term expiring September 8, 2024, and an *automatic renewal* for successive 90-day periods unless either party delivered written termination notice at least 15 days before expiration. The September 25, 2024 Board minutes reflect that management was *uncertain whether a formal written termination notice was sent* before September 8, 2024. If no notice was sent, the exclusivity auto-renewed on September 8, 2024 and may remain live through approximately December 7, 2024 --- potentially barring the Company from signing a definitive agreement with Hargrove. This must be confirmed and cured immediately.
